checks.a_analysis: The content present in the output accurately reflects the source material. While the JSON contains a placeholder object indicating omitted sections, it is a structural note from the pipeline rather than fabricated narrative text, invented section headings, or hallucinated body text.

The source document does not contain explicit contact information like phone numbers, physical addresses, or email addresses. The QR code on page 24 is correctly represented as an image. No fabricated URLs are present in the output.

The output document is missing a significant portion of the source content. Specifically, pages 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, 22, and 23 are entirely absent from the 'sections' array, replaced by an omission placeholder. This results in the loss of approximately 72% of the document's pages and core information, including all detailed use cases and integration scenarios.
checks.b_evidence_source: Pages 4 through 6, 8 through 13, and 15 through 23 of the source PDF, containing detailed information on OneDrive for Business, SharePoint Online, collaboration features, use cases, best practices, and integration scenarios.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections array, element index 4 (between reading_order 3 and 7)

checks.a_analysis: The two tables present in the output (Storage Scope and Best Practices) successfully capture all data points from their respective source pages (7 and 14). Converting the original presentation slide layout into a table format is an acceptable structural change that preserves all informational rows.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: In the sections that were successfully converted (Pages 1, 2, 3, 7, 14, 24, 25), the text content is complete and matches the source slide content. Total page omissions are recorded under Check C3.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The wording in the converted sections maintains the meaning and specific terminology of the source document. No significant changes to meaning-altering words or numerical data were identified in the audit of present sections.

checks.a_analysis: An examination of the provided JSON output and the source PDF images shows no evidence of unintended content duplication. Each slide's content appears once in its corresponding section.

The output fails to preserve the heading hierarchy of the source. Sub-headings present on multiple slides (e.g., 'OneDrive Storage' on page 7, 'Folder Structure & Naming' on page 14) are not captured as semantic heading levels (such as H3 or H4). Instead, they are either placed into table header cells or merged as plain text within data cells. This prevents screen reader users from navigating the document's internal section structure.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 7 contains sub-headings 'OneDrive Storage' and 'SharePoint Storage'. Page 14 contains sub-headings 'Folder Structure & Naming', 'Sharing Governance', etc.
checks.b_evidence_output: In the 'Storage Scope' section, sub-headings are mapped to table headers (cell_type: header). In the 'Best Practices' section (page 14), sub-headings are merged into plain text blocks within data cells (e.g., "text": "Folder Structure & Naming\nMaintain an organized...").

checks.a_analysis: The output introduces tables where none exist in the source document. Pages 7 and 14 utilize a multi-column or grid-like layout for comparisons and lists, but these are not formatted as tables in the PDF. The pipeline has forced this content into a table structure, which changes the semantic organization and reading experience compared to the original document structure.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 7 ('Storage Scope') and Page 14 ('Best Practices') contain formatted blocks of text and headings, not tables.
checks.b_evidence_output: The output generates a table element for both the 'Storage Scope' section and the 'Best Practices' section.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections for 'Storage Scope' (page 7, index 7) and 'Best Practices' (page 14, index 14)

checks.a_analysis: While some content has been converted into tables, the internal reading order of the items within those structures (left-to-right, top-to-bottom) generally respects the logical flow of the source document.
The bulleted list on page 2 ('Agenda Items') is correctly identified and converted into an unordered list element in the JSON.
No clickable links were identified in the source PDF samples provided, and none were incorrectly injected into the JSON output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output correctly translates the visual sequence of the slide deck into a single-column, long-form layout. While the visual style is different (slides vs. web page), the organization of information matches the source. Headings and content blocks appear in the same relative order. The conversion from a fixed slide layout to a responsive HTML layout is executed logically without confusing the meaning.

There are no formal tables in the source PDF. The 'Storage Scope' section (page 7) which compares OneDrive and SharePoint is rendered in the HTML as a clear, two-column grid. This effectively represents the visual comparison intended in the source document. No visual corruption or misalignment is observed.

Several meaningful images, diagrams, and icons present in the source PDF are either missing entirely or replaced by large green placeholder boxes in the HTML output. For instance, the collaboration icons on page 8 and the workflow images on page 21 are replaced by green blocks. Additionally, complex diagrams like the network visualization on pages 11 and 20, the folder structure diagram on page 9, and the QR code on page 24 are missing. Only a few images (like the cloud diagram on page 4/7 and the desk photo on page 5) rendered correctly.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 8: three illustrative icons for collaboration; Page 11/20: network diagram; Page 21: three workflow-related images; Page 24: QR code for follow-up.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML shows three large green rectangles under 'Collaboration' and 'Power Automate Flows'. The network diagram and QR code are entirely absent from their respective sections.
checks.b_evidence_location: Collaboration section, Integration & Extensibility section, Power Automate Flows section, and NC DIT Innovation Summit Follow-Up section.

checks.a_analysis: A thorough review of the HTML output shows no instances of repeated headers, duplicated paragraphs, or recurring blocks of content that were not present in the source PDF. Each slide from the source corresponds to a single section in the output.
